mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5%) triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns falls below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 40% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 3.0%) signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.0%, or the directional relationship reverses (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production (INDPRO < 100) indicates underutilized capacity and deflationary pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate policy easing.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 20d returns in > 50% of INDPRO < 100 instances, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts (HOUST < 1,700k) signals weak demand for durable goods, reducing corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of HOUST < 1,700k instances, or the directional relationship reverses (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 3.5%) indicates labor market instability, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 40% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A yield curve inversion (FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL) signals recessionary expectations, reducing equity risk appetite and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The yield curve inversion fails to produce negative 5d returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
